retrieved_docs.content: B. The hospitals required to report under this section, health care entities, or professional review bodies that provide such information in good faith shall not be subject to suit for civil damages as a result of providing the information. C. A hospital, health care entity or professional review body failing to comply with the reporting requirements provided in this section shall be subject to civil penalty not to exceed $10,000."

retrieved_docs.title: New Mexico Medical Practice Act Review and Understanding -- Issues of Concern -- Section 15. Section 61-6-15 NMSA 1978 (being Laws 1969, Chapter 46, Section 6, as amended) is amended to read:
retrieved_docs.content: B. The board may, in its discretion and for good cause shown, place the licensee on probation on the terms and conditions it deems proper for the protection of the public, for rehabilitation of the probationer, or both. Upon expiration of the term of probation, if a term is set, further proceedings may be abated by the board if the holder of the license furnishes the board with evidence that the licensee is competent to practice, is of good moral character, and has complied with the terms of probation.

retrieved_docs.title: New Mexico Medical Practice Act Review and Understanding -- Issues of Concern -- Section 18. Section 61-6-18.1 NMSA 1978 (being Laws 1994, Chapter 80, Section 10) is amended to read:
retrieved_docs.content: "61-6-18.1. PUBLIC SERVICE LICENSE-- A. Applicants for a public service license shall meet all requirements for licensure and shall: (1) be enrolled in a board-approved residency training program either in New Mexico or in another jurisdiction; (2) obtain written approval from the training program director of the applicant to pursue a public service practice opportunity outside the residency training program; (3) obtain advance written approval from the training program director of the applicant to return to the residency training program following the period of public service; and (4) satisfy other reasonable requirements imposed by the board.

retrieved_docs.title: New Mexico Medical Practice Act Review and Understanding -- Issues of Concern -- Section 19. Section 61-6-19 NMSA 1978 (being Laws 1989, Chapter 269, Section 15, as amended) is amended to read:
retrieved_docs.content: "61-6-19. FEES.-- A. The board shall impose the following fees: (1) an application fee not to exceed $400 for licensure by endorsement as provided in Section 61-6-13 NMSA 1978; (2) an application fee not to exceed $400 for licensure by examination as provided in Section 61-6-11 NMSA 1978; (3) a triennial renewal fee not to exceed $450; (4) a fee of $25.00 for placing a physician's license or a physician assistant's license on inactive status; (5) a late fee not to exceed $100 for physicians who renew their license within 45 days after the required renewal date; (6) a late fee not to exceed $200 for physicians who renew their licenses between46 and 90 days after the required renewal date; (7) a reinstatement fee not to exceed $600 for reinstatement of a revoked, suspended or inactive license; (8) a reasonable administrative fee for verification and duplication of license or registration and copying of records;

retrieved_docs.title: New Mexico Medical Practice Act Review and Understanding -- Issues of Concern -- Section 7. Section 61-6-7 NMSA 1978 (being Laws 1973, Chapter 361, Section 3, as amended) is amended to read:
retrieved_docs.content: D. Physician assistants may prescribe, administer, and distribute dangerous drugs other than controlled substances in Schedule I of the Controlled Substances Act according to rules adopted by the board after consultation with the board of pharmacy if the prescribing, administering, and distributing are done under the direction of a supervising licensed physician and within the parameters of a board-approved formulary and guidelines established under Subsection C of Section 61-6-9 NMSA 1978. The distribution process shall comply with state laws concerning prescription packaging, labeling, and record-keeping requirements. Physician assistants shall not otherwise dispense dangerous drugs or controlled substances.

retrieved_docs.title: New Mexico Medical Practice Act Review and Understanding -- Issues of Concern -- Section 4. Section 61-6-4 NMSA 1978 (being Laws 1989, Chapter 269, Section 4) is amended to read:
retrieved_docs.content: F. Board members shall receive per diem and mileage as the Per Diem and Mileage Act provides. They shall receive no other compensation, prerequisite, or allowance, except that the secretary-treasurer may be additionally compensated as provided in Subsection E of this section, and board members may be additionally compensated in accordance with Subsection G of this section. G. Board members or agents performing interviews of applicants may be compensated at the board's discretion."

retrieved_docs.title: New Mexico Medical Practice Act Review and Understanding -- Issues of Concern -- Section 13. Section 61-6-13 NMSA 1978 (being Laws 1989, Chapter 269, Section 9, as amended) is amended to read:
retrieved_docs.content: C. An endorsement provided under this section shall certify that the applicant has passed an examination that meets with board approval and that the applicant is in good standing in that jurisdiction. In cases when the applicant is board-certified, has not been the subject of disciplinary action that would be reportable to the national practitioner data bank or the healthcare integrity and protection data bank, and has unusual skills and experience that are not generally available in this state, Patients residing in this state have a significant need for such skills and experience; the board may waive a requirement imposing time limits for examination completion that are different from the requirements of the state where the applicant is licensed. D. An applicant for licensure under this section may be required to personally appear before the board or a designated agent for an interview.

retrieved_docs.title: New Mexico Medical Practice Act Review and Understanding -- Issues of Concern -- Section 13. Section 61-6-13 NMSA 1978 (being Laws 1989, Chapter 269, Section 9, as amended) is amended to read:
retrieved_docs.content: A. The board may grant a license without examination and by endorsement to an applicant who is properly endorsed by the officers of the examining board with jurisdiction or by the Canadian Medical Council and: (1) has graduated from a medical college or school in good standing; (2) is board-certified in a specialty recognized by the American Board of Medical Specialties; (3) has been a licensed physician in the United States or Canada and has practiced medicine in the United States or Canada immediately preceding the application for at least 3 years; (4) holds an unrestricted license in another state or Canada; and (5) was not the subject of a disciplinary action in a state or province. B. The board may grant a license by endorsement to an applicant who is properly endorsed by the officers of the examining board with jurisdiction or by the Canadian Medical Council and: (1) has graduated from a medical college located outside the United States or Canada; (2) is of good moral character;

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 2 -- Community 850
retrieved_docs.content: care during pregnancy, labor, and delivery. This integrated framework supports comprehensive obstetric assessment by recognizing how fetal presentation anomalies and maternal surgical history jointly inform the need for specialized care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 3 -- Community 2407
retrieved_docs.content: إجهاض فائت (missed abortion) is linked to ولادة جنين ميت (birth of a dead fetus), indicating that a missed abortion can be associated with the delivery of a non‑viable fetus. Moreover, ولادة جنين ميت is a member of the Standardized MedDRA Query (SMQ) titled إنهاء الحمل وخطر الإجهاض (Termination of pregnancy and risk of abortion), which groups together adverse events related to pregnancy termination and fetal loss. These connections illustrate how missed abortion and stillbirth are interrelated concepts within broader safety‑monitoring frameworks for adverse pregnancy outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2241
retrieved_docs.content: External obstetrical version is recognized as a fetal procedure and is classified within a broader parent category of obstetric interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 898
retrieved_docs.content: temporal occurrence during the fetal period, providing a detailed framework for understanding how diverse maternal and fetal conditions can impact labor and delivery outcomes and guiding clinicians in assessing and managing these complex scenarios.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2232
retrieved_docs.content: The Standardized MedDRA Query titled “Complications and risk factors of pregnancy, labor and delivery (excluding abortion and stillbirth)” includes several specific obstetric conditions. It classifies “حمل بثلاثة توائم، تمت الولادة” (triplet pregnancy that resulted in delivery), “حمل توأمي، تمت الولادة” (twin pregnancy that resulted in delivery), “حمل توأمي، غير محدد لنوبة الرعاية” (twin pregnancy with an unspecified care episode), “تعسر المخاض” (difficult labor), and “رأب عنق الرحم” (cervical insufficiency) as recognized complications or risk factors that can affect maternal and fetal outcomes during pregnancy, labor, and delivery. Grouping these entities together supports comprehensive safety monitoring and clinical assessment of pregnancy‑related risks while explicitly excluding cases of abortion and stillbirth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2138
retrieved_docs.content: Surgical termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل (جراحي)) is classified as a member of the broader clinical grouping “termination of pregnancy and risk of miscarriage” (إنهاء الحمل وخطر الإجهاض (SMQ)). This relationship indicates that the specific procedure of surgically ending a pregnancy is encompassed within the larger safety‑monitoring and terminology framework that addresses both pregnancy termination methods and the associated risk of miscarriage, facilitating comprehensive categorization and assessment in medical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 1 -- Community 232
retrieved_docs.content: with attention to contraindications, and how structured terminologies provide organized frameworks for perinatal and pediatric diagnoses, supporting comprehensive patient assessment and care planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2391
retrieved_docs.content: Abortion, also described as termination of pregnancy, is classified under the term الإجهاض المحرض, which denotes an induced abortion.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 33 -- Level 2 -- Community 658
retrieved_docs.content: Abortion (termination of pregnancy) is classified under the term الإجهاض المحرض (induced abortion). In the hierarchical terminology, abortion (termination of pregnancy) has a parent concept of إنهاء الحمل (جراحي) (surgical termination of pregnancy). The concept إنهاء الحمل (جراحي) is a member of the standardized MedDRA query إنهاء الحمل وخطر الإجهاض (SMQ), which groups terms related to termination of pregnancy and the risk of abortion. Moreover, the SMQ إنهاء الحمل وخطر الإجهاض (SMQ) is itself a member of the broader category ولادة جنين ميت (birth of a dead fetus). This organization links the specific clinical entity of abortion to larger safety and outcome categories, supporting comprehensive pharmacovigilance and patient‑care considerations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 1 -- Community 156
retrieved_docs.content: assistance. Recognizing the hierarchical and cross‑linguistic relationships among these entities supports comprehensive obstetric assessment and targeted management to mitigate maternal and fetal morbidity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 752
retrieved_docs.content: essential for clinicians to anticipate potential delivery complications, decide when normal delivery procedures are feasible, and implement appropriate interventions to optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 1 -- Community 189
retrieved_docs.content: each condition as a potential adverse event, enabling systematic risk assessment, targeted monitoring, operative decision‑making, and coordinated maternal‑fetal and newborn care aimed at reducing morbidity and improving outcomes for both mother and infant.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2393
retrieved_docs.content: External obstetrical version is a fetal procedure that is classified among other delivery procedures, indicating that it is a type of intervention performed on the fetus to reposition it prior to birth and is grouped within the broader category of delivery methods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 3 -- Community 2066
retrieved_docs.content: Multiple pregnancy (حمل متعدد), twin pregnancy that resulted in delivery (حمل توأمي، تمت الولادة), triplet pregnancy that resulted in delivery (حمل بثلاثة توائم، تمت الولادة), and quadruplet pregnancy that resulted in delivery (حمل بأربعة توائم، تمت الولادة) are all designated as members of the Standardized MedDRA Query for complications and risk factors of pregnancy, labor, and delivery, specifically excluding cases of miscarriage and stillbirth. This classification highlights that all forms of multiple gestation leading to live birth are recognized as clinical scenarios associated with increased obstetric complications and risk factors, underscoring the importance of careful monitoring and management in maternal‑fetal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2117
retrieved_docs.content: assessment of complication rates and effectiveness. Trend qualifiers capture evolving usage patterns, technological advances, and guideline updates for all listed interventions, ensuring that clinical decision‑making remains aligned with current best practices. The community also uses an “Allowed” qualifier to indicate that quantitative statistical and numerical data are collected and reported for each of these procedures, and a parallel “trendy” qualifier to designate them as emerging, widely discussed, or of heightened clinical interest. By linking statistical metrics with trend status, the framework supports clinicians, researchers, and health administrators in monitoring performance indicators, evaluating evolving practice patterns, and prioritizing resource allocation for procedures that are both data‑rich and gaining prominence in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 2 -- Community 848
retrieved_docs.content: a more specific subcategory and is linked broadly to “previous c-section,” reflecting the clinical relevance of a prior cesarean section to pelvic organ pathology. Finally, “Previous cesarean section complicating pregnancy or childbirth” is itself a child of the general “previous c-section” category, reinforcing the connection between earlier surgical delivery and subsequent pelvic and cervical complications.
